value.body:
## Cleanup candidate

Theme, density, API host, and repository commit-style literals are defined separately in desktop app config, API response schemas, and API runtime settings types.

## Evidence

- Desktop config schemas: `packages/desktop/src/shared/AppConfig.ts`
  - `ThemePreference = Schema.Literals(["light", "dark", "system"])`
  - `InterfaceDensity = Schema.Literals(["compact", "spacious"])`
  - API host literals
  - `RepositoryCommitStyle = Schema.Literals(["descriptive", "compact"])`
- API HTTP schemas: `packages/api/src/http/schemas/AppConfigResourceEnvelope.ts`
  - same `ThemePreference`, `InterfaceDensity`, API host literals, and `RepositoryCommitStyle` shapes.
- API runtime TypeScript-only aliases: `packages/api/src/http/runtime/CycleApiRuntime.ts`
  - `LocalSettingsThemePreference`
  - `LocalSettingsInterfaceDensity`
  - `LocalSettingsRepositoryCommitStyle`

## Suggested approach

Create or reuse canonical app-settings schemas/types that both desktop and API can import. Prefer schema-derived types over TypeScript-only literal unions at process boundaries, and derive API payload/output schemas from the shared source where practical.

## Acceptance criteria

- Preference and commit-style literal sets are defined in one canonical module.
- API payload/output schemas and runtime provider types are schema-derived or import the same canonical types.
- Existing config parsing, HTTP payload validation, and desktop settings mutations remain compatible.

frontmatter.title: Consolidate local settings preference types and schemas across desktop and API

value.title: Consolidate local settings preference types and schemas across desktop and API
